title: Add summon resource cost
description: Require the summon flow in the C++ multiplayer server to consume configured player resources before creating the summoned unit, while preserving login, matchmaking, status query, and result recording flows.
acceptance_criteria: Summon succeeds only when the player has enough resources.
Resource deduction happens once per successful summon.
Existing login, matchmaking, status query, and result recording code paths are not intentionally modified.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add global summon cooldown
description: Add a global three second cooldown to summon requests in the C++ multiplayer server, rejecting repeated summon attempts inside the cooldown window without changing unrelated request handlers.
acceptance_criteria: A successful summon starts a three second global cooldown for the player or room as specified by the existing design.
Summon attempts during cooldown return a clear failure state.
Cooldown state does not affect login, matchmaking, status query, or result recording flows.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add matchmaking timeout cancellation
description: Cancel a pending matchmaking request after a configured timeout and return the player to an idle state without disrupting active battle rooms.
acceptance_criteria: Pending match requests expire after the configured timeout.
Expired players are removed from the match queue exactly once.
Active rooms and result recording behavior are not modified.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add battle event logging
description: Add structured logging for key battle events such as room creation, summon attempts, cooldown rejection, and battle completion.
acceptance_criteria: Battle logs include room id, player id, event type, and result where available.
Logging failures do not crash the server request flow.
Authentication and persistence code are not intentionally changed.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add status query fields
description: Extend the status query response to include summon resource count, summon cooldown remaining time, and current room state while preserving the existing response contract.
acceptance_criteria: Status responses include resource count, cooldown remaining seconds, and room state when available.
Existing status fields remain present and compatible.
No unrelated login, match, or record flow files are modified.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Fix header include cycle
description: Resolve a circular include problem between battle room and player session headers using forward declarations and source-file includes where appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Headers compile without circular include errors.
Forward declarations are used only where complete types are not required.
Public interfaces remain source-compatible for existing callers.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Fix LNK2019 handler definition
description: Fix an unresolved external symbol caused by a declared but missing or mismatched request handler definition in the C++ server project.
acceptance_criteria: The declared handler has exactly one matching definition.
Any new cpp file required by the fix is included in the Visual Studio project.
No unrelated business logic is changed.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add summon configuration loading
description: Add configuration fields for summon resource cost and cooldown duration, loading them through the existing server configuration path with safe defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Summon resource cost and cooldown duration are read from configuration when present.
Safe defaults are used when configuration fields are missing.
Configuration parsing failures are reported without crashing normal startup.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add room state enum
description: Introduce an explicit room state enum for waiting, matching, active, finished, and closed states, then use it in room status reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Room state values are represented by a strongly typed enum.
Status reporting maps enum values to stable protocol strings or values.
Existing room lifecycle transitions remain intact.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.

title: Add simple server simulation test
description: Add a lightweight simulation test for login, matchmaking, summon, status query, and result recording using existing test or debug harness conventions.
acceptance_criteria: The simulation covers login, match entry, summon attempt, status query, and result recording at a high level.
The test avoids external network or database dependencies unless an existing fake is available.
Production code changes remain limited to enabling testability where required.
The isolated workspace build succeeds.
